extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won stage 4 with dominant sprint after RICKAERT Jonas and VAN DER HOORN Taco stayed away longest from early break
tactical_patterns: DE BUYST Jasper delivered final leadout turn for EWAN despite working extensively earlier, revealing Lotto depth advantage
RICKAERT Jonas outperformed higher-placed breakaway companions on Ardennes circuit, signaling selective climbing strength over endurance riders
Lotto sprint train regrouped after split before 8km despite MERLIER Tim dropping, exposing rival teams' inability to force selection

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: Pure speed advantage evident with multi-bike-length margin despite hilly Ardennes finale suggests form peaking pre-Tour
rider_signals.DE BUYST Jasper: positive: Delivered high-quality final turn after extended work indicates exceptional diesel engine for hilly sprint stages
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: Dropped before final 8km on Ardennes circuit while slower sprinters survived suggests weight/climbing vulnerability
rider_signals.RICKAERT Jonas: positive: Last breakaway survivor alongside VAN DER HOORN on selective Ardennes terrain despite being GC-focused rider
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Acknowledged course insufficiently hard for GC splits, revealing tactical awareness of terrain limitations for attacking
